etymology.description: 美(美) (měi, "beauty; beautiful") refers to what is pleasing visually or aesthetically, capturing a sense of attractiveness and elegance. 麗(麗) (lì, "beautiful; splendid") emphasizes brightness and attractiveness, suggesting something that stands out due to its visually striking quality. Together, 美麗(美麗) forms a compound that emphasizes the quality of being visually pleasing and strikingly attractive.
etymology.interpretation: 美(美) (měi, "beauty; beautiful") evokes attractiveness and aesthetic appeal, which leads to 'beautiful'. 麗(麗) (lì, "beautiful; splendid") implies brightness and striking appearance, linking to vivid beauty. Together, 美麗(美麗) signifies something that possesses both elegance and vivid visual appeal, rightfully describing 'beautiful'.

examples.chinese: 这座美丽的花园令人心醉(這座美麗的花園令人心醉)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zuò měilì de huāyuán lìngrén xīnzuì.

examples.chinese: 她的笑容非常美丽(她的笑容非常美麗)。
沿着这条美丽的小路漫步(沿著這條美麗的小路漫步)，能让人心情愉悦(能讓人心情愉悅)。
examples.pinyin: Yánzhe zhè tiáo měilì de xiǎolù mànbù, néng ràng rén xīnqíng yúyuè.
examples.english: Strolling along this beautiful path can make one feel delighted.
